interactive.name: PICT-inspired GCD Pick for 2-wise when row count matters. It produced the fewest pairwise rows in the current comparison samples.
AETG-style Pick when you can spend more runtime to chase compact output. It was strongest for 4-wise row count in our samples.
Compatibility graph Pick for comparison against graph-style compatibility scoring. It was usually behind the best row-count choices.
Hypergraph vertex Pick as the graph-informed comparator that stays closer on rows than compatibility graph, with higher runtime.
Bach AllPairs Pick for 2-wise when you want Bach Allpairs-style pair-frequency balancing. It targets lower row counts than the simple legacy
Pairwise (simple) Pick for legacy-compatible 2-wise output. It is stable and familiar, though PICT and AETG used fewer rows in our sample ru
Greedy Pick as the general default for low rows with low runtime. It led our 3-wise and 5-wise samples overall.
IPOG-style Pick for predictable low runtime at higher strengths. It tracked Greedy closely and tied it on some larger samples.
Cartesian Product Will generate 81 rows to cover (3*3*3*3) combinations.
